task_completion.description: Verify the USLCI database has been imported into the LCA software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'uslci_database' or similar USLCI entries in the active databases navigation panel of the LCA software (e.g., openLCA).

task_completion.description: Verify a new unit process named 'Global Workbench Assembly' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a process tab, window, or tree node explicitly named 'Global Workbench Assembly'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Wood and Steel components were added as inputs with correct masses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Inputs/Outputs table for the 'Global Workbench Assembly' process. There should be an entry for Wood with an amount of 50 kg and an entry for Steel with an amount of 5 kg.

task_completion.description: Verify the transport work was correctly calculated and added as inputs in ton*km (t*km).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Inputs/Outputs table. There should be a truck transport flow with an amount of 17.0 t*km (or very close, within tolerance) and an ocean transport flow with an amount of 40.0 t*km.

task_completion.description: Verify the product system was calculated using the TRACI 2.1 impact assessment method.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a calculation setup window, results tab, or impact assessment configuration showing 'TRACI 2.1' selected as the LCIA method.

task_completion.description: Verify the calculation results were exported to the specified CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog, a success notification, or a file explorer window showing 'workbench_results.csv' located in the '~/LCA_Results/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual LCA software GUI to perform the modeling.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the interface of an LCA application (like openLCA) with its standard panels (navigation, editor, properties), rather than a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV export was generated by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the software's native export dialogs or calculation result windows, ensuring the agent didn't just manually type a mock CSV file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the inputs were entered into the software's data tables.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values (50 kg, 5 kg, 17.0 t*km, 40.0 t*km) should be visible inside the software's Input/Output flow tables, not just written in a standalone script or terminal echo.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Process Name: 'Global Workbench Assembly'. Material Inputs: Wood (50 kg) and Steel (5 kg). Transport Work Inputs: Truck transport must be 17.0 t*km (calculated as 50kg*300km + 5kg*400km) and Ocean transport must be 40.0 t*km (calculated as 5kg*8000km). Impact Assessment Method: TRACI 2.1. Export destination: ~/LCA_Results/workbench_results.csv.
